title: A Theremin Model
description: A basic theremin model built using Arduino

modalDetails.description:
If you don't know: A theremin is an electronic musical instrument that you play without physically touching it. (Dont worry I didnt know about it earlier either.)

Working:

I made this project to represent our music club in an event. It was made in like a 1 night’s time so its pretty basic and not exactly like how a real theremin works.
The LDR and resistor form a voltage divider circuit. When the light intensity changes based on how you wiggle your hands above it, the resistance of the LDR changes, causing a change in voltage. The Arduino reads this voltage through its analog pin and converts it into different frequencies, which are then played through the buzzer.

![Equation](/assets/theremin_equation.png)

The Big challenge:

At first, the circuit was not sensitive enough because I was using a 220 Ω resistor, so it only ran when a flashlight was given right above it and not with just background light.

 I sat down, read the code again and again trying to see if I could make some small changes to get it working, but no amount of change was making the sensitivity better, the pitch and the overall model was getting better but it still needed a flashlight to run.
 Then when I was explaining what I was making to one of my friends they asked me what was the actual physics behind it and then I figured out the equation and I just got to know I just had to amplify the fixed resistance to a much bigger value to increase sensitivity.

 The whole time I was trying to find the solution at the wrong place, am glad I got it working at last.
